name: Abnb
description_short: ABNB Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution offering personal and business banking, loans, mortgages, and investment services with 24/7 online access.
description_long:
ABNB Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union regulated by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration). Based on their website presence and routing number (251481627), ABNB serves members seeking an alternative to traditional for-profit banks with competitive rates and member-focused products.

ABNB offers comprehensive personal banking services including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), money market accounts, and Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs). Their lending portfolio includes auto loans (as low as 4.99% APR), personal loans, credit cards (Visa Preferred from 10.24% APR and Visa Signature from 12.74% APR), home mortgages, and home equity lines of credit (HELOCs) ranging from 5.75% to 7.24% APR depending on term. They also provide business banking through their ProBiz line, offering business checking, savings, loans, credit cards, lines of credit, and commercial real estate financing.

ABNB distinguishes itself through member-centric features including 24/7 online banking, instant transfers with no enrollment needed, support for digital payment platforms (Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay), and direct deposit services. They offer investment services and wealth advisory appointments alongside financial education resources. Their digital infrastructure emphasizes accessibility (AudioEye enabled website) and security, with fraud protection and ID theft resources prominently featured.

As a credit union, ABNB provides member benefits typical of the cooperative model, though specific membership eligibility requirements are not detailed on their public website. Their rates are competitive but not universally the lowest—CD rates (3.00%-3.20% APY) and auto loan rates (4.99%) are moderate rather than exceptional. Physical branch access is limited (delayed openings noted), though 24/7 online banking mitigates this.
services: Certificates of deposit (9-month at 3.00% APY, 12-month at 3.20% APY)
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) up to 3.25% APY
Performance money market accounts up to 1.35% APY
Auto loans for new and used vehicles from 4.99% APR
Boat and RV loans from 8.99% APR
Visa Preferred and Visa Signature credit cards
Home equity lines of credit (7, 12, and 17-year terms)
Business loans and lines of credit
pros: 24/7 online banking and account access through digital branch
Instant transfers available 24/7 with no enrollment required
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 4.99% APR for new and used vehicles
HELOC options with multiple term lengths (7, 12, 17 years) and rates from 5.75% APR
Support for major digital payment platforms (Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay)
Comprehensive business banking services (ProBiz) including commercial real estate loans
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure with NCUA insurance protection
cons: Limited information on membership eligibility criteria on public website
Delayed physical branch openings indicate limited in-person service availability
CD rates (3.00%-3.20% APY) are moderate, not among the highest available
Savings account APY rates not explicitly stated on main website
No mention of fee structures, minimum balance requirements, or overdraft policies
best_for: Members seeking 24/7 digital banking with instant transfer capabilities
Small business owners needing comprehensive commercial banking and lending services
Homeowners interested in HELOC products for debt consolidation or home improvement
Consumers prioritizing member-owned, not-for-profit financial institutions over corporate banks
diagnosis: ABNB Federal Credit Union is best suited for members who prioritize digital banking convenience, want member-owned not-for-profit benefits, and seek comprehensive personal and business lending services. The main caveat is that membership eligibility may be restricted (typical for credit unions), and physical branch availability appears limited, making this primarily a digital-first institution.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Abnb — Credit Union in Chesapeake, VA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Abnb — NCUA-insured credit union in Chesapeake, VA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
